description_long: This is a medium-sized brick train station building with a gabled roof and a prominent central entrance. The structure features a main two-story section with decorative stonework at the corners and a smaller, single-story annex attached to one side. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with dark shingles, and a brick chimney rises from the main section. Windows are symmetrically arranged, with white frames contrasting against the brown brick facade. A sign above the main entrance displays the station's name, and the overall design suggests a late 19th or early 20th-century European architectural influence.
description: A brown brick train station with a gabled roof, central entrance, and attached annex, featuring white-trimmed windows and a station name sign.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the annex extending from the main building, with the gabled roof and brickwork visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the annex, the main building's height, and the chimney.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the main entrance, symmetrical windows, and the annex to the right.
description_view_3: Oblique front view showing the entrance, station sign, annex, and roof details.
attribute_platform_canopies: One large, rectangular canopy attached to the left side of the building, constructed with a dark roof and wooden supports, providing shelter along the annex.
attribute_station_signage: One prominent station name sign above the main entrance, displaying 'NIEZDROWICE' in bold letters.
